reasoning: The community prediction on Metaculus for this question was 5% on November 13, 2025, reflecting a very low probability close to resolution. My searches confirm that the CDC's most recent estimates (derived from the 2019 Threats Report and subsequent pandemic-era updates) place the annual US death toll from antibiotic-resistant infections around 35,000 to 48,000. There have been no new credible reports or CDC updates in 2024 or 2025 estimating that 70,000 or more people die annually in the US. With less than six weeks remaining until the end of 2025, and no expected major US-specific reports identified that would drastically double the accepted baseline figure, the resolution is extremely likely to be NO.
